title: Good Debt vs Bad Debt
summary: Not all debt is the same: some borrowing can help build long-term value while other borrowing funds things that quickly lose value. This lesson explains the difference for first-time earners.

sections.heading: The Idea of Good Debt
sections.body: Good debt is borrowing that can help you build value or income over time, such as an education loan that improves your earning ability or, in some cases, a home loan for an appreciating asset. The key idea is that what you bought may grow in value or boost your future income. Such debt is usually larger but carries a longer-term purpose.

sections.heading: The Idea of Bad Debt
sections.body: Bad debt is borrowing to fund wants that lose value quickly, such as the latest gadget or an expensive holiday put on a high-interest credit card. The item often depreciates while the interest charges keep growing. Carrying a balance on high-interest debt month after month can quietly drain your finances.

sections.heading: Judging Any Debt
sections.body: Whether debt is helpful depends on the interest rate, what you are buying, and whether you can comfortably repay it. Even education or home loans can become a burden if the repayments stretch you too thin. Asking whether the borrowing builds future value or just funds a passing want is a useful first test.

quiz.prompt: Which is the clearest example often described as 'good debt'?
options: A high-interest loan for a luxury holiday
An education loan that improves earning ability
Credit-card balance for a new phone

quiz.prompt: A high-interest credit-card balance carried for many months is a problem because:
Even an education or home loan can become a burden if:
options: The repayments stretch you too thin

quiz.prompt: A useful first test for any borrowing is to ask whether it:
options: Builds future value or just funds a passing want

disclaimer: Educational content only. This is a simulation, not financial advice.
